geopolitics_summary: Middle East conflict and Strait of Hormuz crisis elevate EU energy security concerns; UK sanctions rollback signals geopolitical realignment.

themes.headline: EU growth forecasts cut as Middle East war pressures energy prices
themes.why_it_matters: Ongoing Middle East conflict directly threatens crude and LNG supply stability; European Commission confirms energy price inflation risk. TTF and EUA curve should reprice upward on persistent geopolitical premium.

themes.headline: Europe records record May temperatures; heat dome intensifies heatwave risk
themes.why_it_matters: Early, intense heatwaves boost near-term power demand (cooling) but may depress hydroelectric supply in southern EU; net effect depends on reservoir status. Watch DA spreads and intraday peaks; Cal+1 downside if hydro output impaired.

themes.headline: US adds 44.9 Bcf/d pipeline capacity 2026–2027; 70% from Texas
themes.why_it_matters: Increased US domestic pipeline capacity may boost LNG export optionality, improving NW European supply options and dampening TTF upside. Indirect: lower US gas price → higher US LNG competitiveness → reduced EU LNG spot exposure.

themes.headline: Data center server energy use projected to rise significantly across US
themes.why_it_matters: Structural US power demand growth (AI, data centers) may constrain US LNG exports or raise US generation costs, indirectly supporting TTF and European power prices via tighter global LNG market.

themes.headline: UK signals rollback of Russia sanctions; EU expresses concern
themes.why_it_matters: UK sanctions relief could theoretically ease Russian energy re-entry to global markets; however, EU maintains embargo. Risk of fragmented Western sanctions reduces long-term price support for EU energy independence narrative; monitor Russian LNG/pipeline restoration talks.

watchlist: NATO high-level Strait of Hormuz security discussion (late May 2026); assess geopolitical oil/LNG supply risk.
EU energy competitiveness ministerial debate in Brussels; watch for grid/market design reforms affecting EUA, forward curves.
US pipeline capacity Q3–Q4 2026 commissioning; monitor LNG export growth and TTF arbitrage windows.
Data center AI-driven demand trajectory; track EU power demand revisions and renewable adequacy assessments.
